description: Multi-location promotional campaigns with time-limited offers and buy-one-get-one deals distributed across branch networks, announced via single Instagram posts with Arabic captions.
structural_recipe: Post a single carousel or image-based post announcing a week-specific promotional offer valid across all branches. Structure: (1) Headline with product name and emoji, (2) Clear BOGO or discount mechanics, (3) Size specifications or variants covered, (4) Campaign duration/validity dates, (5) Optional: Repeat key details. Use Arabic captions with friendly tone and celebratory emojis (🥰, 😊). Include product imagery showing the featured item.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers value transparency and clarity on offer mechanics, particularly for F&B where size/portion variations matter. Multi-branch announcements build confidence that deals are widely accessible rather than location-exclusive. Limited-time framing (weekly rotation) creates urgency and encourages immediate visits. BOGO psychology resonates strongly in Middle Eastern retail culture.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure Arabic captions are grammatically correct and culturally appropriate—typos reduce credibility
Must clearly state validity dates in Islamic calendar or Gregorian; ambiguity reduces conversion
BOGO offers must be genuinely available across stated branches to avoid damaging brand trust
Emoji usage should align with brand tone; excessive or mismatched emojis may appear unprofessional
cultural_constraints: Promotions during Ramadan require sensitivity to fasting hours and timing
Avoid imagery or language that conflicts with local values or religious observances
Pricing should reflect local purchasing power expectations
